summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2024-003, a request for an area variance to allow construction of a new single-family home at 155 square feet less than the 1,400 square foot minimum required by the R3 zoning district. The applicant, Robin Fleming, sought to replace a fire-damaged home on Parker Road with a 1,245 square foot three-bedroom, two-bathroom ranch. Staff recommended approval noting the variance was only 1% beyond the administrative approval threshold and consistent with surrounding neighborhood character. The board unanimously approved the variance.
decisions: Approved video minutes from the June 5, 2024 meeting (5-0)
Approved area variance BZA 2024-003 for a 155 square foot reduction from the 1,400 sq ft minimum for a new single-family dwelling (5-0)
action_items: Robin Fleming to coordinate with zoning staff on next steps to move forward with construction
Zoning staff to accept new applications for the next meeting by August 1st deadline
key_amounts: 1,400 square feet: minimum floor area required for single-family homes in R3 district
155 square feet: variance reduction requested (11% reduction from requirement)
930 square feet: existing damaged ranch home on the property
10%: administrative approval threshold for deviance from standards
notable_topics: Area variance request for undersized single-family home (BZA 2024-003)
Replacement of fire-damaged home on Parker Road
Neighborhood character and compatibility with surrounding smaller homes
Property maintenance violation on the subject property since the fire
Proposed home setbacks and site placement
attendees: Max Merritt (Zoning Consultant for Goshen Township)
Pam Hodge (Neighboring property owner, 1719 Parker Road)
notes: Mr. Cegla was absent from the meeting. The property had an existing fire from 2020 and an ongoing property maintenance violation. The applicant mentioned plans to convert to electric, remove an old feral gas tank, and improve driveways/culverts with neighbors. Six of 14 properties within 200 feet are already under the 1,400 sq ft minimum. The 11% variance was noted to be only 1% beyond the 10% administrative approval threshold.
title: BZA 7-15-24
